mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not precede negative 60-day forward returns more than 55 percent of the time, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising federal funds rates fail to compress equity valuations because nominal earnings growth offsets discount rate increases, resulting in neutral to positive forward returns over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate profit bases, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a twenty-day window as earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth is not positively correlated with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of positive GDP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding five hundred thousand jobs signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, causing equity drawdowns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS exceeds 144000, or the threshold signal yields a positive Rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces real personal savings and credit expansion, it indicates supply-side bottlenecks driving profit margins higher, generating positive equity returns over a sixty-day horizon.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO to RSAFS does not predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term fails to explain variance in equity returns beyond single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, stable federal funds rates prevent a credit crunch, allowing equity markets to rally on the expectation of imminent policy support over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not positive when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent and FEDFUNDS remains flat, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample testing.
